(**NFA**) is the governing body of [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") in [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"). The [South West Africa](/wiki/South_West_Africa "South West Africa") Football Association had organized multiracial football since 1976.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 1990, it was reorganized as the Namibia Football Association, which later affiliated with [FIFA](/wiki/FIFA "FIFA") and [CAF](/wiki/Confederation_of_African_Football "Confederation of African Football") in 1992. It organizes the [national football league](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_Football_League "Namibia Premier Football League") and the [national team](/wiki/Namibia_national_football_team "Namibia national football team"). Its aim is to create a football culture and industry that provides entertainment and economical benefits for all; to become a dominant national association within the confederation via the professionalism of the game's administration; to promote education and development programmes in all aspects of the sport.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Governing body of association football in Namibia

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This article **contains [promotional content](/wiki/Wikipedia:What_Wikipedia_is_not#Wikipedia_is_not_a_soapbox_or_means_of_promotion "Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not")**. Please help [improve it](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Namibia_Football_Association&action=edit) by removing [promotional language](/wiki/Wikipedia:Spam "Wikipedia:Spam") and inappropriate [external links](/wiki/Wikipedia:External_links#Advertising_and_conflicts_of_interest "Wikipedia:External links"), and by adding encyclopedic text written from a [neutral point of view](/wiki/Wikipedia:Neutral_point_of_view "Wikipedia:Neutral point of view").  See our **[advice if the article is about you](/wiki/Wikipedia:About_you "Wikipedia:About you")** and read our **[scam warning](/wiki/Wikipedia:Scam_warning "Wikipedia:Scam warning") in case someone asks for money** to edit this article. *(March 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Namibia Football Association

| [CAF](/wiki/Confederation_of_African_Football "Confederation of African Football") | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Short name | NFA |
| Founded | 1990 |
| [FIFA](/wiki/FIFA "FIFA") affiliation | 1992 |
| [CAF](/wiki/Confederation_of_African_Football "Confederation of African Football") affiliation | * 1990 (Associate member)[[1]](#cite_note-1) * 1992 |
| Website | [nfa.org.na](https://nfa.org.na/) |

The **Namibia Football Association** (**NFA**) is the governing body of [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") in [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"). The [South West Africa](/wiki/South_West_Africa "South West Africa") Football Association had organized multiracial football since 1976.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 1990, it was reorganized as the Namibia Football Association, which later affiliated with [FIFA](/wiki/FIFA "FIFA") and [CAF](/wiki/Confederation_of_African_Football "Confederation of African Football") in 1992. It organizes the [national football league](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_Football_League "Namibia Premier Football League") and the [national team](/wiki/Namibia_national_football_team "Namibia national football team"). Its aim is to create a football culture and industry that provides entertainment and economical benefits for all; to become a dominant national association within the confederation via the professionalism of the game's administration; to promote education and development programmes in all aspects of the sport.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

On 22 July 2020, the NFA officially expelled the [Namibia Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_League "Namibia Premier League") as a member and a 2 year absence of soccer comes from the disputes of these two bodies, and founded the [Namibia Premier Football League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_Football_League "Namibia Premier Football League") in 2021.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## The Leagues
